titleStmt.title: Letter from the Crown Solicitor, Cavan, to Sir Matthew Nathan, 1 March 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Louis C P Smith

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: This is a letter from Louis C.P. Smith (1867-1923) the Crown Solicitor of Cavan, to Sir Matthew Nathan (1862-1939) Under-Secretary for Augustine Birrell (1850-1933) the Chief Crown Secretary of Ireland. In the letter Smith is telling Nathan his side of a of a story after Nathan received a complaint from a Mr. Newell about the delay of Smiths work. Smith claims it is due to the cases happening a considerable time ago.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers, CSO RP/1916/4061

correspAction.placeName: Crown Solicitor's Office, County Cavan, Ireland
Chief Secretary's Office, Dublin Castle, Dublin, Ireland

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
CROWN SOLICITOR'S OFFICE,
CAVAN 1st day of March 1916.
No 9278: 1916.
County of Cavan. Sir/- I am in receipt of a letter from
the Local Government Board to the Under Secret-
ary of the 28th ulto. and with reference to
the portion of same which you say I am to reply
to, I have to state, that on the 10th February
I received a letter from Mr Newell asking to
have certain answers given to queried which
he furnished to me. I found considerable diff-
iculty in complying with some of them as they
occurred some considerable time ago. I made
inquiries through the Police for the purpose of
ascertaining how the respective amounts set
out in the queries were made up, and have
just received replies, and have to-day sent
on same to Mr Newell. I had a letter from
this gentleman on the 24th ult. asking for
these answers, and replied to him on same day
explaining the delay. Yours faithfully
M. Kelly
Crown Sol.
Cavan
To The Under Secretary.
Chief Secretary's Office.
The Castle.
DUBLIN.
